toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to run tests with `dotnet test` and collect a crash dump if any test crashes the host process. 

To collect crash dumps on .NET, the standard approach is to use environment variables:
- `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1` — enables mini dumps on crash
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4` — full dump (MiniDumpWithFullMemory)
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=<path>` — where to write the dump

Let me run the tests with these environment variables set.

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-3603f9f10efe43e4828848c7b6d2248b && \\\nDOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1 \\\nDOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4 \\\nDOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=\"/tmp/sv-3603f9f10efe43e4828848c7b6d2248b/crashdump_%p.dmp\" \\\ndotnet test --no-pager 2>&1", "description": "Run dotnet test with crash dump collection", "initial_wait": 60}

toolArgs.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

data.content:
Tests passed. Here's the command I used:

```bash
DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1 \
DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4 \
DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName="/tmp/sv-3603f9f10efe43e4828848c7b6d2248b/crashdump_%p.dmp" \
dotnet test
```

**What these environment variables do:**
- **`DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1`** — enables automatic crash dump generation when the test host crashes
- **`DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4`** — captures a full memory dump (use `1` for a smaller mini dump)
- **`DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=..._%p.dmp`** — sets the dump file path (`%p` is replaced with the process ID)

All tests passed and no crashes occurred, so no dump files were created.
